# DeStore

DeStore builds GS1 Digital Link QR code infrastructure and Digital Product Passport (DPP) issuance services for Australian brands exporting to the EU.

## What we do
- Issue GS1 Digital Link QR codes for physical products — each physical unit gets its own unique QR code and permanent DPP record
- Migrate existing product catalogues to GS1 Digital Link compliance in 48 hours
- Issue Digital Product Passports (DPPs) as required by EU ESPR (Ecodesign for Sustainable Products Regulation)
- Anchor DPP records to Ethereum mainnet for permanent, tamper-evident verification
- Support Shopify, WooCommerce, Magento, spreadsheets, and any structured data format

## The problem we solve
From July 2027, the EU's ESPR regulation requires a Digital Product Passport on every physical product sold into Europe. No passport — no EU market access. Traditional GS1 migration takes 6-12 months and costs $15,000-$80,000+. DeStore does it in 48 hours at a fixed price.

## Key facts
- 48-hour delivery guaranteed
- 7-day money-back guarantee if not satisfied
- Fixed price quoted before work begins
- No developer required
- Each physical unit (e.g. each pair of shoes) gets its own unique GS1 Digital Link QR code
- Works with any existing product data format

## Who we serve
Australian brands selling physical products into the EU, required to comply with ESPR by July 2027. Particularly apparel, footwear, skincare, homewares, electronics, and sporting goods brands.

## Pricing
Free assessment. Fixed price quoted after reviewing your catalogue scope. 7-day money-back guarantee.

## Technology
- GS1 Digital Link QR codes (ISO/IEC standard)
- Base (Coinbase's Ethereum Layer 2) for DPP issuance
- Ethereum mainnet anchoring for permanent verification
- Laava Smart Fingerprint authentication
- OriginTrail integration

## Location
Byron Bay, Australia

## Contact
josiah@destore.network
https://destore.network
